summary.rationale: The third refinement is the strongest comparison result: the triangle gains enough presence without returning to a generic geometric scale, while the independently shorter and higher minor dash reads quickly as a chord quality mark.

notes: All three requested refinements have screenshot evidence.
Existing state coverage, score semantics, responsive density, and width fit remain intact.
Independent optical values reflect the different recognition needs of a triangular outline and a horizontal dash.
Root, quality, and extension continue to form a compact scan unit on representative mobile rows.
Iteration 1 was correctly rejected as slightly heavy and iteration 2 exposed the limitation of shared metrics.
Iteration 3 achieves restrained, engraving-specific proportions on both mobile and desktop.
The result is grounded in the existing music notation system and actual rendered score rather than generic UI typography.
The per-glyph correction is small, explicit, and limited to the dedicated quality symbols.
